problem: helix-cli (hlx) provides production inspection capabilities for databases, logs, and APIs, plus ticket comment management. For the 'Monitoring with Auto Solve' feature, helix-cli is the tool through which the monitoring agent probes Helix Global production environments and posts monitoring status updates as ticket comments.

files.why: Database inspection command. Sends POST to /api/inspect/:repoId/database with SQL query. The monitoring agent uses this to run read-only production database checks (e.g., verifying record states, counting error records).
Log fetching command. Sends POST to /api/inspect/:repoId/logs with query and optional limit. The monitoring agent uses this to search production logs for expected success/error patterns.
API inspection command. Sends GET to /api/inspect/:repoId/api. The monitoring agent can check health endpoints or API responses.
Lists available repositories and their inspection capabilities (types: DATABASE, LOGS, API). The monitoring agent needs to discover which repos can be inspected.
Posts agent comments to tickets. The monitoring agent posts status updates (check passed, check failed, waiting for data) as comments. Server determines isAgentAuthored flag.
Lists ticket comments with filtering (--helix-only, --since). Monitoring agent can review prior status updates and human feedback.
HTTP client with 3 retries, exponential backoff, 30s timeout, and rate-limit handling. All inspection and comment calls use this resilient client.
Configuration management supporting HELIX_API_KEY, HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN, HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY env vars. Config file at ~/.hlx/config.json.
Repository resolution by ID, name, or partial match. Monitoring agent resolves repo names to IDs for inspection calls.
CLI entry point defining all commands: login, inspect (db/logs/api/repos), comments (list/post), --version.
Package: @projectxinnovation/helix-cli v1.2.0. Binary: hlx. Build: tsc. Typecheck: tsc --noEmit. Zero runtime dependencies.

facts: CLI binary: hlx. Published as @projectxinnovation/helix-cli v1.2.0. Zero runtime dependencies (pure Node.js built-ins).
Inspection commands: hlx inspect db --repo <name> '<SQL>', hlx inspect logs --repo <name> '<query>' [--limit N], hlx inspect api --repo <name> <path>.
Comment commands: hlx comments list [--ticket <id>] [--helix-only] [--since <iso-date>], hlx comments post [--ticket <id>] '<message>'.
Auth: supports HELIX_API_KEY, HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN, HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY env vars. Also ~/.hlx/config.json file auth. hxi_ prefixed keys use X-API-Key header.
Ticket ID resolution: from --ticket flag or HELIX_TICKET_ID environment variable (injected by Helix during sandbox execution).
HTTP resilience: 3 retries, exponential backoff (2s base), retry on 429/500/502/503/504, 30s timeout, Retry-After header support.
Build: TypeScript compilation (tsc). Typecheck: tsc --noEmit. No lint or test scripts defined.
The CLI is already used inside Helix workflow sandboxes. HELIX_TICKET_ID and inspection tokens are injected as environment variables during step execution.
unknowns: Whether helix-cli needs new monitoring-specific commands (e.g., 'hlx monitor check', 'hlx monitor plan') or if existing inspect and comments commands are sufficient for the monitoring agent.
Whether the monitoring agent will invoke hlx as a subprocess or use the server's inspection-proxy-service directly. Both paths exist.
Whether the --limit parameter on log queries is sufficient for monitoring or if additional filtering (e.g., time-window, severity) should be added to the CLI.
